title: Markets are closed — but this week’s low-priced setups proved the process works
author: Zealousideal-Hat4801
selftext:
Markets are closed, but I spent some time reviewing this past week — and honestly, I’m pretty satisfied with how things played out

Not chasing big names this time. Most of the focus was on lower-priced stocks with cleaner setups — looking for consistency rather than hype

This week’s results (+15.11%) didn’t come from guessing tops or bottoms, but from sticking to a simple process:  
  
Watching how price reacts around short-term moving averages  
  
Paying attention to volume shifts instead of noise  
  
Letting confirmation come before entry

I’ve been slowly incorporating more structured, almost “rule-based” thinking into my trading (inspired by AI-style logic — less emotion, more process), and it’s starting to make a difference

Still a long way to go, but weeks like this remind me that consistency > chasing spikes

To this end, a friend and I have personally compiled a set of strategies and methods for analyzing market trends, which we are sharing within my group. If you are interested, please leave a comment or contact me directly. My hope is to assist those who can truly benefit from this guidance

comments.author: Independent-Tea-1667
comments.body: I'm interested. Are you willing to share it with me?

replies.author: Zealousideal-Hat4801
replies.body: I would be very happy to
